message:
test(fcp-conformance/revocation-push): pin two-tier signing transcript binding (br-zpihj)

RevocationPushMessage carries two independent signatures with
deliberately different transcripts:

- owner_signing_bytes: zone_id || revoked_ids || new_rev_seq, with
  from/timestamp OMITTED so the owner sig stays valid across every
  forwarding peer at every forwarding time. handle_revocation_push
  relies on this to relay pushes without re-attestation (br-uxsnk).
- signing_bytes: owner content PLUS from + timestamp, so each
  forwarder authenticates the delivery envelope itself.

revocation_timing.rs constructs these messages but did not pin the
asymmetric transcripts. A regression that mixed from/timestamp into
owner_signing_bytes would silently break cross-peer portability; one
that dropped from/timestamp from signing_bytes would let an attacker
re-attribute a captured push.

Adds 11 tests against the public fcp_mesh::gossip API:

- owner_signature_round_trip_passes_verify_owner_signature
- node_signature_round_trip_passes_verify_signature
- owner_signing_bytes_excludes_from_so_signature_is_portable_across_forwarders
- owner_signing_bytes_excludes_timestamp_so_signature_is_portable_in_time
- owner_signing_bytes_binds_to_zone_id
- owner_signing_bytes_binds_to_revoked_ids
- owner_signing_bytes_binds_to_new_rev_seq
- node_signing_bytes_bind_to_from (forwarder attribution)
- node_signing_bytes_bind_to_timestamp (replay defense)
- owner_sig_under_wrong_key_is_rejected
- revoked_ids_order_is_part_of_the_owner_transcript (the documented
  caller-side ordering contract)

All 11 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
revocation_push_signing_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
